question: What is “banking” on a pattern?
content:
BANKING IS WHEN YOU USE THE OUTSIDE FRICTION AS A “BOUNCE BOARD” FOR YOUR BALL
ON PATTERNS WHERE THE OUTSIDE PART OF THE LANE HAS STRONGER FRICTION AND THE INSIDE HAS HEAVIER OIL, THE LANE NATURALLY CREATES A BUILT IN MISS ROOM:
MISS A LITTLE RIGHT → THE FRICTION “BANKS” THE BALL BACK TOWARD THE POCKET
MISS A LITTLE LEFT → THE OIL INSIDE HOLDS THE BALL SO IT DOESN’T OVER HOOK.
WHEN BOTH OF THESE HAPPEN TOGETHER (FRICTION OUTSIDE + HOLD INSIDE) YOU GET A SHAPE WHERE THE LANE ACTS LIKE A CHANNEL THAT GUIDES YOUR BALL INTO THE POCKET.
